meta.notes: Read-only research log. No trades are ever placed by this routine.

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: 78 of 80 required events reported as of company's May 11, 2026 update; final analysis triggers on 80th event; topline readout guided for 2H 2026
catalyst.next_check: watch for 8-K / press release announcing 80th event

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 69.8% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold

catalyst.name: $25B investment-grade bond deal (priced late Jun/early Jul 2026, $89B demand, proceeds retired bridge loan) + staggered IPO lockup
catalyst.status: First lockup tranche (20% of 180-day block) unlocks 2nd full trading day after Q2 2026 earnings, expected late Jul/early Aug 2026; further 7% tranches Aug-Oct; full lockup expires Dec 8 2026
catalyst.next_check: Q2 2026 earnings date announcement; stock reaction into lockup

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within cap but second-largest single-name risk; lockup supply event ~2-3 weeks out

flags: SLS at 69.8% of NAV is nearly double the 35% single-name trim threshold, in a pre-binary-catalyst biotech with no news that breaks the thesis but no catalyst confirmation either.
Zero USD cash buffer - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).
SPCX facing its first staggered lockup unlock in ~2-3 weeks; stock already down 4-5% pre-market with no negative credit news (bond was oversubscribed, investment-grade).

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: Still 78 of 80 required events as of early-July 2026 CEO commentary; CEO signaled this may be his last public post before entering quiet period ahead of the 80th event; no new event/unblind announcement found

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 71.5% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold (worsened vs 69.8% on 7/11 as SPCX fell faster)

catalyst.name: $25B bond deal + staggered IPO lockup
catalyst.status: DETERIORATED: stock down ~38% from June 16 peak ($225.64) and down ~25% since IPO per 7/10 Forbes piece; the $25B bond is now reportedly 'trading like junk' despite investment-grade rating at issuance; George Noble (public bear) pegs fair value at $30/share citing bubble valuation; Morgan Stanley flagged China's Long March 10B booster recovery as a competitive threat; Bernstein SocGen maintained Outperform/$239 PT as a counterpoint. First lockup tranche (20% of 180-day block) still expected 2nd full trading day after Q2 2026 earnings (late Jul/early Aug)
catalyst.next_check: Q2 2026 earnings date announcement; bond spread trend; stock reaction into lockup

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - credit/valuation narrative turned materially more negative since 7/11; no longer just a supply-overhang story

flags: SLS concentration worsened to 71.5% of NAV (from 69.8%) purely from SPCX's decline, not new SLS-specific news; REGAL still stuck at 78/80 events with no fresh disclosure.

SPCX down another 5% since the 7/11 run, with new negative credit-market commentary (bond 'trading like junk') replacing the prior run's 'no negative credit news' read - this is a material change to the catalyst thesis.
Zero USD cash buffer persists - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).
Both single-day gaps vs prior close are small (SLS +0.55%, SPCX -0.38%) - today's premarket move is muted; the deterioration is cumulative over the past 3 days, not an overnight shock.
